nodes.name: Webhook
nodes.notes: Set the app's N8N_WEBHOOK_URL to this node's Production URL. Create a 'Header Auth' credential named to match, with Name=Authorization and Value=Bearer <N8N_WEBHOOK_TOKEN> — never hardcode the token in this workflow JSON, only in the n8n credential store.

assignments.name: smsText

nodes.name: Build booking SMS text
nodes.notes: Round 2026-07-26 fix: reads ONLY from $json.body (the webhook payload) — no $env, this n8n instance blocks environment access inside nodes. The dashboard domain is a plain literal placeholder here (not a secret, just not known at workflow-authoring time) — replace REPLACE_WITH_CLINIC_DOMAIN once, after import. Recipient number and Melipayamak credentials live on the HTTP Request node downstream, not here — see docs/n8n/README.md.

assignments.name: smsText

nodes.name: Build urgent SMS text
nodes.notes: Same fix as 'Build booking SMS text' — payload fields only, no $env.

assignments.name: smsText

nodes.name: Build status SMS text
nodes.notes: Optional per the spec ('appointment.status_changed: Optional internal SMS') — disabled by default. Enable this node + the HTTP Request node after it if you want a text on every status change; that can get noisy for a small clinic. Same no-$env fix as the other two Build nodes.

nodes.name: Melipayamak: send booking SMS
nodes.notes: Round 2026-07-26 fix: `username`/`password`/`from` no longer come from $env or from a Build node — they're injected by the 'Melipayamak Credentials' Custom Auth credential attached to this node (see docs/n8n/README.md §4). `to` is a plain static placeholder — replace REPLACE_WITH_SECRETARY_MOBILE with the real number after import. Free-text SendSMS endpoint (distinct from the app's own pattern-based OTP endpoint) — verify the exact field names/response shape against your live Melipayamak panel/docs before relying on this in production.

nodes.name: Melipayamak: send urgent SMS (secretary)
nodes.notes: isflash=true requests a flash SMS where the provider/handset supports it — a reasonable 'higher-priority' signal; drop it if your Melipayamak line doesn't support flash. Same credential-based auth as 'send booking SMS' — see docs/n8n/README.md §4.

nodes.name: Melipayamak: send urgent SMS (owner)
nodes.notes: Urgent requests notify BOTH the secretary and the owner — a genuinely urgent/safety case (e.g. a broken-nose trauma request) is deliberately over-notified rather than risk being missed.

nodes.name: Melipayamak: send status-change SMS
nodes.notes: Disabled by default, matching 'Build status SMS text' above — this is the optional branch.

nodes.name: Unmatched event (no-op)
nodes.notes: Any event name this workflow doesn't recognize lands here and is simply dropped — the app is expected to only ever send the 3 documented event types (see docs/n8n/README.md).
